threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'excuse me' on the bus, like it's a shop queue. Around here, it's an elbow-first affair.
Last Saturday on the 133, a bloke started singing '90s R&B, but only the choruses. Everyone joined in at the stops, it was like karaoke on wheels.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old mattress as a makeshift trampoline in the park, I knew this place had a spirit of its own.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that serves cocktails in old soda bottles—it's like drinking history with a twist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know how to dodge the BART delays and still make it to a taco truck before the lunch rush hits. Tourists? They’re still stuck at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 31 Balboa at 5 PM, passengers just staring, phones out. It’s like a living art gallery—totally San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into any bar and get a proper brunch. Here, brunch is a more civilized affair, often starting after 11 am.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewilderment at the confusing metro map—especially when they’re staring at the signs, trying to make sense of "L1" and "L3".

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look at the Tube map with hope instead of resignation, like it won’t derail your plans every other weekend.
Last night on the 35, a guy in a full crocodile onesie tried to convince everyone he was on a mission from 'the swamp.' Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When your neighbor borrowed your bike pump and returned it with a freshly baked Zopf. That's pure Neukölln hospitality, right there.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here will you find a bus driver who takes a coffee break mid-route, leaving passengers to chat about their plans. It's oddly charming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local can spot a Mission burrito by the way someone holds it—like they're cradling a newborn, not just another meal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy serenading the 14 bus stop crowd with a ukulele. Only in SF do you get spontaneous concerts while waiting for a ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every corner café serves great coffee. Local spots often hide in plain sight, pouring the best brews while the busy streets lure in the unsuspecting.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their fascination with the local birds. They stop mid-conversation, eyes wide, gushing over sparrows like they've never seen one before.
